---
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: true

title: "Markov Chain Example - MIT 18.642"
format:
  pdf:
    output-file: "mit18_642_f24_lec05_1 - R code MC_Example1"
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  html:
    output-file: "mit18_642_f24_lec05_1 - R code MC_Example1"
    embed-resources: true
    image-format: "png"
    toc: true
    toc-location: "left-body"
    number-sections: true
---

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
\newpage
:::

# Lecture 5 - MIT 18.642 - Markov Chain Example

## Load packages (and install if necessary)

```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

library(quantmod)
# Load package or Install if needed
if (!requireNamespace("markovchain", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("markovchain", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
}

library(markovchain)
library(diagram)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyquant)
# Load igraph package for graph analysis
library(igraph)
```

## Load Apple stock data

```{r}
#1. Collect/load daily data on stock


# Check if object AAPL does not exist in the environment
if (!exists("AAPL")) {
  
  # Download AAPL stock data using tidyquant
  AAPL <- tq_get("AAPL")
  
  # Save the AAPL object to an RData file
  save(file = "mit18_642_f24_lec05_1 - MC_Example1_stockData.RData",
       list = c("AAPL"))
}

# Load the AAPL object from the RData file
load(file = "mit18_642_f24_lec05_1 - MC_Example1_stockData.RData")
```

## Plot stock price

```{r}
#2. Plot Data
ggplot(AAPL,
       aes(x=date,y=close)
       ) +
  geom_line() +
  ggtitle("AAPL")
```

```{r}
AAPL0 <- AAPL |> 
  dplyr::filter(date >= as.Date("2020-01-01")) |> 
  dplyr::filter(date <= as.Date("2024-08-31")) 
str(AAPL0)
```

```{r}
df.aapl <- data.frame(AAPL0)
str(df.aapl)
```

## Markov Chain

```{r}
y <- df.aapl$close
# Define difference in closing price and 2 lags
ydiff.lag0 <- y-LAG(y)
# LAG (dif)
ydiff.lag1 <- LAG(ydiff.lag0)
# LAG(LAG(dif))
ydiff.lag2 <- LAG(ydiff.lag1)

# 2-Day State Definition 
statevec2DAY <- 10*(ydiff.lag1>0) + 1*(ydiff.lag0>0)
length(statevec2DAY)

# State Vector
state2DAY <- as.character(statevec2DAY)
state2DAY <- ifelse(statevec2DAY==11,"UU",state2DAY)
state2DAY <- ifelse(statevec2DAY==10,"UD",state2DAY)
state2DAY <- ifelse(statevec2DAY==01,"DU",state2DAY)
state2DAY <- ifelse(statevec2DAY==00,"DD",state2DAY)
table(state2DAY)

```

```{r}
state2DAY <- factor(state2DAY,
                    levels = c("DD","DU","UD","UU"),
                    ordered = TRUE)

df.aapl$state2DAY <- state2DAY

df.aapl |>  
  dplyr::filter(date >= as.Date("2024-05-01")) |>
  ggplot(aes(x=date,y=state2DAY)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line(aes(x=date,y=as.numeric(state2DAY))) +
  ggtitle(
    paste0(
    "Markov Chain: AAPL stock\n",
    "State: UD = Up Day (t-1) and Down Day (t)"
    )
  )

```

## Build transition probability matrix

```{r}
# Create lagged version of the previous period state
state2DAY.LAG1 <- LAG(as.numeric(state2DAY))

# Build transition count matrix: rows = previous state, cols = current state
nmat <- table(state2DAY.LAG1,state2DAY)

# Align row names with column names for consistency
dimnames(nmat)[[1]]<- dimnames(nmat)[[2]]
# Display transition matrix
nmat
```

```{r}
# Normalize each row to obtain transition probabilities
pmat <- diag((rowSums(nmat))^-1) %*% nmat

# Align row names with column names for consistency
dimnames(pmat)[[1]]<- dimnames(pmat)[[2]]

# Display transition probability matrix rounded to 2 decimals
print(round(pmat,digits = 2))
```

## Plot Markov Chain graph

```{r}
# Figure : Plot Markov Chain Graph
mc <- as(pmat,"markovchain")

plot(mc,main="Markov Chain: AAPL States")

```

```{r}
# Convert Markov chain object to igraph object
mathematica.igraph <- as(mc, "igraph")

# Compute strongly connected components (modern igraph function)
SCC <- components(mathematica.igraph, mode = "strong")

# Assign a color to each vertex based on its component membership
V(mathematica.igraph)$color <- rainbow(SCC$no)[SCC$membership]

# Create list of vertex groups (one per strongly connected component)
groups <- split(V(mathematica.igraph), SCC$membership)

# Plot graph highlighting communicating classes
plot(mathematica.igraph,
     mark.groups = groups,
     main = "Communicating classes:\n strongly connected components")
```
